phases.name: Slice 01 — shareable /survey page -> anonymous store -> dashboard read (US-01, US-02, US-05 read, US-08)
phases.description: Website repo. EXTENDS 5123's live Supabase platform per ADR-040/042/046. Hidden /survey renders question config, submits via the NEW service_role submit-survey Edge Fn (response write + per-submission team email), readable in a dashboard survey tab. NO Lighthouse app change.

steps.name: Survey content module + pure answer types
criteria: features/survey/content/surveyContent.ts exports a zod-validated single-select question config for Q1 team-count, Q2 role, Q3 discovery-channel, Q4 assessment-interest (options per feature-delta Survey questions table); types derived via z.infer
A core answer type (SurveyAnswers) represents one selected option per question; no scoring, no score/raw_sum/band fields exist anywhere in the survey core

surveyContent.test.ts validates the config against the production zod schema (factory getMockSurveyAnswers(overrides?) validated, not redefined) and asserts every question has >=2 options and a stable id
core contains no @supabase / window / I/O imports (functional-core purity, ADR-035 idiom)
bun run test green; bun run build clean; eslint . clean on the new files
steps.scenario_name: survey content config is schema-valid and renders the confirmed questions (survey-page.feature support)
steps.implementation_notes: WEBSITE repo /storage/repos/website (bun NOT pnpm; CI bun build-only; lint ESLint NOT Biome; React 19 + Vite 7 + TS loose-strict + zod). EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: TS-row of the polyglot matrix governs (Vitest + zod), NOT the Python/Hypothesis pilot; no assert_state_delta/Universe. MIRROR 5123's assessmentContent.ts idiom (read /storage/repos/website/src/features/assessment/content/assessmentContent.ts first): a single zod-validated content module is the SSOT for questions; editing it never touches the route (US-03 capability, hardened in slice 02). Questions are CONFIRMED in feature-delta 'Survey questions' (Q1 Just-mine/2-5/6-10/>10; Q2 roles list; Q3 channels list; Q4 interest scale or yes/no - accommodate either with a discriminated option list, see open question Q4). NO email/exchange field in slice 01 (that is slice 03). Use a factory getMockSurveyAnswers(overrides?) validated against the schema; never redefine the schema in tests. Strict TS, no any (narrow unknown via guard); immutable; <=2 nesting; no comments. CI-LEARNINGS PRE-APPLY: grep docs/ci-learnings.md for the TS/website families; skip framework-only tests (assert business behaviour). RED handoff: write surveyContent.test.ts asserting schema validity + option invariants and confirm RED-for-right-reason (module/exports missing) before implementing; one RED->GREEN->REFACTOR; refactor commit separate.

steps.name: Hidden /survey route renders from config
criteria: A /survey route is added to App.tsx ABOVE the catch-all, rendering the survey flow from surveyContent (intro -> single-select questions -> submit), no login/account required
Reaching /survey directly and via a (simulated) shared link render the identical page
If the question config fails to load/validate, the page shows a graceful 'survey temporarily unavailable' message, never a blank page

/survey is HIDDEN: no nav/footer link added, not in any sitemap, NO robots.txt Disallow added; .github/workflows/deploy.yml SPA fallback copies dist/index.html to dist/survey/ (mirror the /assessment fallback)
RTL component tests cover render-from-config + the unavailable fallback; bun run test green; bun run build clean; eslint clean
steps.scenario_name: Visitor lands on /survey and sees the current questions with no login (survey-page.feature)
steps.implementation_notes: WEBSITE repo; TS-row exempt. MIRROR 5123's assessment components (IntroStep/QuestionStep) + App.tsx route registration + the deploy.yml SPA-fallback step (read .github/workflows/deploy.yml: 5123 added cp dist/index.html into dist/assessment/ and dist/admin/assessment/; add dist/survey/ the same way). The submit action is injected as a port (a no-op/stub double in this step's tests) - the real submit-survey adapter lands in 01-03; do NOT wire Supabase here. Render single-select questions from surveyContent (config-driven, NOT hardcoded) so slice 02 can prove editability without a route change. Graceful-unavailable = catch a content load/validate failure and show a calm message (failure_mode from journey step-land-on-survey). HIDDEN route: confirm no Navigation entry + no sitemap edit + NO robots Disallow (robots is public -> would advertise the path; see ADR-043). Use react-hook-form + the existing shadcn primitives like 5123. Strict TS no any; immutable; <=2 nesting; no comments. CI-LEARNINGS PRE-APPLY. RED handoff: render test asserting questions appear from config + the unavailable fallback; confirm RED-for-right-reason (route/components missing) before implementing; one RED->GREEN->REFACTOR.

steps.name: Ports widening + submit-survey edge fn (anonymous response write)
criteria: ports widened: ResponseSource union admits 'user-survey'; a SurveySubmission.submit(answers) driving port + EdgeFunctionSurveySubmission adapter invoke the new submit-survey function; 5123's assessment ResponseSource/CapturedResponse non-null-scored invariant stays intact (regression test)

supabase/functions/submit-survey/index.ts (service_role) validates the payload, rejects stray score/band, inserts the anonymous response into 5123's responses table (source='user-survey', answers jsonb, raw_sum/score/band = null) and returns recorded:true

The survey response is written via service_role only; responses_anon_insert is NOT widened to 'user-survey' (a structural test / the absence of any 0003 migration asserts the anon policy stays 'readiness-assessment'-only)

Thank-you is shown ONLY on a confirmed successful write; a write failure shows a retry-able error with answers preserved and NO thank-you; a double-submit records one logical response (idempotent/dedup)
submit-and-store.feature in-memory scenarios pass; @real-io live-POST scenarios stay describe.skip (no website CI creds); bun run test green; bun run build clean; eslint clean
steps.scenario_name: Visitor submits and the anonymous response is stored; thank-you only on success; retry-able error; dedup (submit-and-store.feature)
steps.implementation_notes: WEBSITE repo; TS-row exempt (Deno edge fn is plain TS). ADR-040 + ADR-046 are authoritative; the slice-01 brief predates ADR-046 (it said anon-INSERT) - FOLLOW ADR-046: the write goes through the service_role submit-survey Edge Fn, NOT the anon key, because every submission must also trigger the team email (01-04). MIRROR 5123's supabase/functions/capture-lead/index.ts (CORS, createClient with SUPABASE_URL + SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, parsePayload fail-closed, 400 on bad shape) and src/features/assessment/adapters/* + ports/index.ts. Read those first. Widen ResponseSource in ports/index.ts to 'readiness-assessment' | 'user-survey' and add a SurveySubmission port + the survey adapter (invoke('submit-survey', { body })); keep CapturedResponse's non-null scored shape for the assessment and add a guarded non-scored survey shape (ADR-040) - add a regression assertion that the assessment path still requires score/band. submit-survey inserts source='user-survey', answers jsonb, raw_sum/score/band null; reject any payload carrying score/band (fail closed). Dedup: a client-side idempotency guard (disable submit after first success / single in-flight) is sufficient for slice 01 (note dedup limits if server-side later). Degrade/thank-you: thank-you ONLY after recorded:true; on non-200 show retry-able error and keep answers in the form (journey failure_modes). The team email is 01-04 (do not add Mailgun here yet; submit-survey gains it next step). 5123 platform IS live (responses table exists). Strict TS no any; immutable; <=2 nesting; no comments. CI-LEARNINGS PRE-APPLY (System.Text.Json N/A here; watch ESLint + TS-strict). RED handoff: in-memory capture test (injected SurveySubmission double) asserting thank-you-on-success / error-on-failure / dedup; confirm RED-for-right-reason before implementing; one RED->GREEN->REFACTOR; @real-io live POST stays describe.skip.

steps.name: Per-submission team notification email (degrade-open)
criteria: supabase/_shared/surveyNotificationEmail.ts renders a team alert (subject + text/html) summarising the answers; recipient constant survey.answer@letpeople.work
submit-survey sends ONE notification via the existing _shared/mailgun.ts transport after the response is recorded; a NO-opt-in submission states 'no trial requested' and carries NO email address
The send is degrade-open: a Mailgun failure is caught/logged and the submission still returns recorded:true and the visitor still sees the thank-you (mirror 5123 sendResultsEmail)
Unit tests cover the renderer (answers summary + 'no trial requested') and a degrade test (send throws -> submission still succeeds); a parity test guards the answer-label mirror if any
@real-io live-POST contract stays describe.skip; bun run test green; bun run build clean; eslint clean
steps.scenario_name: A submission notifies the team; no-opt-in alert states no trial and carries no email; Mailgun failure is degrade-open (submit-and-store.feature US-08)
steps.implementation_notes: WEBSITE repo; TS-row exempt. ADR-046 authoritative. MIRROR 5123's supabase/_shared/resultsEmail.ts (Deno-canonical pure renderer) + _shared/mailgun.ts (readMailgunConfig((k)=>Deno.env.get(k)) | sendViaMailgun(config,{to,subject,text,html,replyTo})) - read both first; REUSE mailgun.ts, do NOT add a new transport or dependency. The renderer is TEAM-facing (an ops alert), distinct from the visitor results email: subject like 'New Lighthouse survey response'; body = a readable answers summary + a trial line. Slice 01 has NO trial opt-in, so the line is always 'No trial requested' and NO email is included (the email + 'Trial requested - <email>' branch lands in slice 03). AI-5 (resolved): when the trial branch arrives, answers + email go together in one alert, no submission-time notice - but that is slice 03; slice 01 only emits the no-trial form. Degrade-open: wrap the send in a non-throwing helper exactly like 5123's sendResultsEmail (try/catch, console.error, return); submit-survey still returns 200/recorded:true. Recipient survey.answer@letpeople.work is a const in surveyNotificationEmail.ts. USER MANUAL STEP (AI-2): the inbox + Mailgun secrets are provisioned out-of-band; the @real-io live POST test stays describe.skip until creds. Strict TS no any; immutable; <=2 nesting; no comments. CI-LEARNINGS PRE-APPLY. RED handoff: renderer + degrade unit tests RED-for-right-reason first; one RED->GREEN->REFACTOR.

steps.name: Dashboard survey view (read) + summarizeSurvey
criteria: core/summarizeSurvey.ts (pure) reduces survey responses to per-question option tallies + a total count; NO band distribution (the survey has no bands)
A survey tab/section is added to 5123's AdminDashboard reusing its Supabase Auth + Card/Table shell; DashboardRepository.load('user-survey') returns the survey responses (authenticated SELECT)
The maintainer opens the survey view and sees responses listed/tallied, anonymous (no PII column populated); unauthenticated access returns no data (reuses 5123 auth fail-closed)
The walking-skeleton scenario passes end-to-end (in-memory composition): shared link -> /survey -> submit -> recorded -> team notified -> maintainer sees it in the survey view
dashboard-survey-view.feature in-memory scenarios pass; @real-io authenticated-SELECT stays describe.skip; bun run test green; bun run build clean; eslint clean
steps.scenario_name: Maintainer sees survey responses tallied and anonymous in the dashboard survey view (dashboard-survey-view.feature + walking-skeleton.feature)
steps.implementation_notes: WEBSITE repo; TS-row exempt. ADR-042 authoritative (dashboard survey view STANDS; only the RLS-widen seam was superseded). MIRROR 5123's core/dashboardSummary.ts (summarizeDashboard) + adapters/supabaseDashboardRepository.ts + components/AdminDashboard.tsx - read first. CREATE NEW summarizeSurvey (NOT reuse summarizeDashboard: the survey has no bands/scores -> band-distribution reuse renders a misleading all-zero grid; ADR-042 evidence). It computes per-question option tallies + total. EXTEND DashboardRepository.load to accept the 'user-survey' source (already parameterized by source in 5123) and the dashboard to render a survey tab beside the assessment view, reusing the existing Supabase Auth (benjamin@/peter@) + Card/Table primitives (no new auth, ADR-033). Responses are anonymous (no email column for survey responses; the trial-requests view is slice 03). Add a structural guard that summarizeDashboard/bandOfScore is never called on a survey (null-band) response. This step closes the walking-skeleton loop in-memory. Strict TS no any; immutable; <=2 nesting; no comments. CI-LEARNINGS PRE-APPLY. RED handoff: summarizeSurvey unit tests (tallies) + the dashboard render test RED-for-right-reason first; un-skip the walking-skeleton scenario LAST and confirm it goes green; one RED->GREEN->REFACTOR.

steps.name: UX revision: one-question-per-screen survey reusing a shared stepper
criteria: A shared generic presentational stepper component (string-valued options, configurable total/progress/next-label) is extracted; both the assessment and the survey render through it
The assessment's QuestionStep delegates to the shared component (or uses it directly) with NO change to its observable behaviour; all existing assessment tests stay green

The survey steps ONE question per screen (intro -> Q1..Q4 advancing on Next/selection -> submit), with a progress indicator and Back navigation, replacing the all-on-one-page SurveyForm; a survey step reducer (core/surveyMachine.ts) drives phase/index/answers
Submit (thank-you-on-success / retry-able error / dedup from 01-03) fires at the end of the stepped flow; survey answers preserved across Back/Next
bun run test green (assessment + survey); bun run build clean; eslint clean
steps.scenario_name: Visitor advances one question per screen and submits at the end (survey-page.feature one-at-a-time)
steps.implementation_notes: WEBSITE repo; TS-row exempt. USER UX CORRECTION 2026-05-31: the survey currently renders all questions on one page; it must match the assessment's ONE-QUESTION-PER-SCREEN flow and REUSE that component (jump to the next page after each answer). READ FIRST: src/features/assessment/components/QuestionStep.tsx (the presentational card: progress bar + RadioGroup options + Back/Next) and core/quizMachine.ts (the intro->question->teaser reducer). EXTRACT a SHARED generic presentational stepper (e.g. src/components/quiz/QuestionStepCard.tsx) with props { prompt; options:{value:string;label:string}[]; questionNumber; totalQuestions; selected:string|null; progressLabel; nextLabel?; onSelect(value:string); onBack; onNext }. The assessment's QuestionStep becomes a thin adapter over it (its options.value is numeric -> map to String() and parse back on select; QUESTION_COUNT -> totalQuestions; last-button 'See my result') so the assessment's observable behaviour + tests are UNCHANGED (regression guard: assessment suite green). DO NOT force-share quizMachine - the survey gets its own core/surveyMachine.ts mirroring quizMachine's shape but with phases intro->question->submit (no scoring/teaser), answers as readonly (string|null)[] of length = survey question count, actions start/answer/next/back/submit, progress 'N of 4'. Rewrite SurveyForm to drive surveyMachine: render the shared stepper one question at a time, advance on Next (selection enables Next; mirror the assessment UX exactly), Back navigation preserves prior answers, and on the last question's submit run the existing SurveySubmission port flow (thank-you-on-success / retry-able error / dedup from 01-03). Update Survey.flow.test.tsx + Survey.capture.test.tsx for the stepped flow (still enter through the public render, assert observable per-screen behaviour). This is a refinement commit on top of shipped slice 01 (push after verify). Strict TS no any; immutable (reducer returns new state, no mutation); <=2 nesting, early returns; no comments. CI-LEARNINGS PRE-APPLY. RED handoff: surveyMachine.test.ts (advance/back/submit/answer-preservation) + the stepped flow test RED-for-right-reason first; keep the assessment green throughout; one RED->GREEN->REFACTOR; the shared-component extraction + assessment delegation is the REFACTOR (separate concern from the survey behaviour - keep assessment tests green at every step).

phases.name: Slice 02 — questions editable without a link change (US-03)
phases.description: Harden the questions-as-config guarantee end-to-end: edit surveyContent, /survey serves the new questions at the same URL, old shared links still resolve, dashboard reads old + new responses.

steps.name: Editable-questions capability + link-stability guard
criteria: Editing surveyContent changes /survey's rendered questions with no route/URL change; a structural test asserts the route constant is independent of the content module
Old responses (prior question set) and new responses both remain readable in the dashboard survey view (no schema break for the survey kind)
bun run test green; bun run build clean; eslint clean
steps.scenario_name: Questions are editable without a link change (survey-page.feature)
steps.implementation_notes: REFINE WHEN REACHED. Mostly a guarding/capability slice on top of slice 01's config-driven render; likely few/no new production files. Confirm the route constant + content-module separation; assert old+new response read-compat.

phases.name: Slice 03 — trial opt-in (US-04, US-05 trial view, US-08 trial branch)
phases.description: Optional email-for-trial exchange screen; submit-survey writes a leads row (source='user-survey-trial', wants_trial) via service_role; team alert now carries the email; dashboard gains a trial-requests view. No auto-issuance (D4).

steps.name: Trial opt-in capture (email -> leads) + no auto-issue
criteria: Optional opt-in + valid email -> submit-survey writes a leads row (source='user-survey-trial', wants_trial=true, email; score/band null) via service_role; no opt-in -> NO email stored anywhere
Opt-in never creates a license automatically (D4); invalid email -> friendly inline validation, rest of answers preserved
The team alert now includes 'Trial requested - <email>' alongside the answers (AI-5); no submission-time privacy notice
trial-optin.feature in-memory scenarios pass; @real-io stays describe.skip; bun run test green; build clean; eslint clean
steps.scenario_name: Trial opt-in records a lead + email, no auto-issue, invalid email validated (trial-optin.feature)
steps.implementation_notes: REFINE WHEN REACHED. ADR-046 trial branch; mirror 5123 leads handling + EMAIL_PATTERN. Partial-write (response ok, lead failed) -> surface trial-specific failure (journey failure_mode).

steps.name: Dashboard trial-requests view
criteria: The dashboard survey view lists trial requests with their emails (authenticated SELECT on leads source='user-survey-trial')
Reuses 5123 auth/layout; no platform redesign (D6); bun run test green; build clean; eslint clean
steps.scenario_name: Maintainer sees trial requests with emails (dashboard-survey-view.feature)

steps.name: Dashboard answer visualizations + date-range filter
criteria: The survey dashboard view renders simple per-question visualizations (recharts bar charts via the existing shadcn chart component) of the answer tallies, beside/instead of the raw table
A date-range filter (last 7 / 30 / 90 days / all time) scopes the survey view; changing it recomputes the tallies + charts client-side from the loaded responses' createdAt
The trial-requests list is also scoped by the same filter; 'all time' shows everything; default is a sensible range (e.g. 30 days)
No new dependency (recharts already present); UTC-stable date math (no inline new Date() in a useEffect dep — useMemo-stabilize per the React #185 lesson)
bun run test green; bun run build clean; eslint clean
steps.scenario_name: Maintainer filters the survey view by date range and sees answer visualizations (dashboard-survey-view.feature)
steps.implementation_notes: USER REQUIREMENT 2026-05-31: the dashboard should show the answers with SIMPLE visualizations + a date filter (last XX days or everything). recharts + src/components/ui/chart.tsx ARE already in the project — REUSE them, NO new dependency. READ FIRST: src/features/survey/core/summarizeSurvey.ts (per-question OptionTally), src/features/assessment/components/AdminDashboard.tsx (the SurveyView added in 01-05 + the trial-requests sub-section from 03-02), src/components/ui/chart.tsx, and how the assessment band-distribution is charted if at all. Build a PURE core `filterByDateRange(responses, range, now)` (range = '7d'|'30d'|'90d'|'all'; now passed IN as a param — do NOT call Date.now() inside; UTC-stable) that returns the responses whose createdAt is within the window; the dashboard recomputes summarizeSurvey on the filtered set. Add a `SurveyCharts` component rendering one simple bar chart per question from the OptionTally data (recharts BarChart via the shadcn ChartContainer). Wire a range selector (shadcn Select/ToggleGroup) into AdminDashboard's survey view defaulting to 30 days; the same range scopes the trial-requests list. CRITICAL React #185 lesson (memory project_react185_loop_unstable_useeffect_dep): a `new Date()` default feeding a useEffect dep caused an infinite re-render that blanked the metrics view — so compute `now` once (useMemo/module constant at render) and NEVER put an unstable Date in a useEffect dependency array. Strict TS no any; immutable; <=2 nesting; no comments. CI-LEARNINGS PRE-APPLY. RED: filterByDateRange unit tests (windowing boundaries, 'all') + a dashboard charts/filter render test RED-for-right-reason first; one RED->GREEN->REFACTOR.

steps.name: Wire the survey dashboard view into the live admin page
criteria: AdminAssessment.tsx (the production /admin/assessment container) loads the 'user-survey' source in addition to 'readiness-assessment' and passes surveyResponses + surveyTrialRequests + a stable now to AdminDashboard
After sign-in the maintainer ACTUALLY sees the survey view (tallies + charts + date filter + trial-requests) on the live page - closing the TBU gap latent since 01-05
A test renders AdminAssessment with an injected repository returning BOTH assessment and survey data and asserts the survey sections render (port-to-port through the page)
Assessment view unchanged/green; bun run test green; bun run build clean; eslint clean
steps.scenario_name: Maintainer sees the survey view on the live admin page (walking-skeleton.feature wiring)
steps.implementation_notes: TBU FIX (orchestrator wiring smoke check). The survey dashboard (SurveyView tallies 01-05, trial-requests 03-02, charts+filter 03-03) is built in the AdminDashboard COMPONENT + tested with injected doubles, but the production PAGE container AdminAssessment.tsx only loads source='readiness-assessment' and never passes survey props - so the survey view is INVISIBLE in prod. READ FIRST: src/pages/AdminAssessment.tsx (loads DASHBOARD_SOURCE then summarizeDashboard + passes summary to <AdminDashboard>), src/features/assessment/components/AdminDashboard.tsx (optional props surveyResponses/surveyTrialRequests/now drive the date-filtered summarize+charts itself), supabaseDashboardRepository.load('user-survey') (returns surveyResponses + surveyTrialRequests). FIX: in AdminAssessment, after/alongside the assessment load, ALSO repository.load('user-survey'); pass the RAW surveyResponses + surveyTrialRequests + a stable now to AdminDashboard (the component does the filter/summarize/charts when raw responses are supplied - pass RAW, not pre-summarized). Compute now once via useMemo(()=>new Date().toISOString(),[]) - React #185 safety, never in a useEffect dep. Keep the assessment path untouched (its summarizeDashboard guard throws on null-band rows - load survey via the 'user-survey' branch which returns surveyResponses, NEVER feed survey rows to summarizeDashboard). Guard active-flag like the existing effects. Strict TS no any; immutable; <=2 nesting; no comments. CI-LEARNINGS PRE-APPLY. RED handoff: extend AdminAssessment.test.tsx - injected repository returns assessment + survey data, assert BOTH the assessment summary AND the survey sections (tallies/charts/trial-requests testids) render after sign-in; RED-for-right-reason (survey sections absent) first; one RED->GREEN->REFACTOR.

steps.name: Survey answers visual-only + consolidate admin dashboard route
criteria: The survey dashboard view shows answers ONLY as charts (the per-question text tally TABLE from 01-05 is removed); the total-responses count, the date-range filter, and the trial-requests list remain
The admin dashboard is served at /admin/dashboard (canonical); /admin/assessment redirects to it (preserve old bookmarks) and /admin redirects to it; deploy.yml SPA fallback covers /admin/dashboard
The assessment view (band distribution + captured-lead emails) is KEPT unchanged on that page (user decision: it is the only surface for assessment leads)
SEO/page title reflects a unified admin dashboard (not 'Assessment results' only)
bun run test green; bun run build clean; eslint clean
steps.scenario_name: Survey answers render only as charts under the consolidated admin dashboard route (dashboard-survey-view.feature)
steps.implementation_notes: USER FEEDBACK 2026-05-31 (after live review): (1) survey answers appear TWICE - a text tally table (SurveyView, 01-05) AND charts (SurveyCharts, 03-03); keep ONLY the visual (charts). (2) Keep the ASSESSMENT dashboard (it is the only surface for assessment leads - no team email for those). (3) Serve everything under a unified /admin/dashboard route. READ FIRST: src/features/assessment/components/AdminDashboard.tsx (the SurveyView text table + SurveyCharts + SurveyTrialRequestsView + the range filter), src/App.tsx (current /admin/assessment route + the /assessment etc. routes; how redirects are done with react-router v7 - use <Navigate replace> or a redirect route), src/pages/AdminAssessment.tsx (SEO title 'Assessment results'), .github/workflows/deploy.yml (the SPA fallback step that cp dist/index.html into dist/assessment, dist/admin/assessment, dist/survey - add dist/admin/dashboard). CHANGES: (a) In AdminDashboard remove the per-question survey TALLY TABLE rendering (SurveyView's table) but KEEP SurveyCharts, the total-responses count, the date-range filter control, and SurveyTrialRequestsView; the assessment band-distribution + leads sections stay untouched. (b) App.tsx: add canonical route path '/admin/dashboard' rendering the existing admin page; redirect '/admin/assessment' -> '/admin/dashboard' (Navigate replace) and '/admin' -> '/admin/dashboard'; keep them all working. (c) deploy.yml: add the dist/admin/dashboard SPA fallback mirroring the existing dist/admin/assessment one (keep the old one too for the redirect shell). (d) Update the page SEO/title to e.g. 'Admin dashboard'. Do NOT rename the page file (low-value churn) - just repoint the route + title. Keep the assessment path/summarizeDashboard exactly as-is. Update the survey dashboard tests to assert the table is gone and the charts remain; update any routing test. Strict TS no any; immutable; <=2 nesting; no comments. CI-LEARNINGS PRE-APPLY. RED: a test asserting the survey text table is absent while charts/total/filter/trials render + (if present) a routing assertion that /admin/dashboard serves the dashboard and /admin/assessment redirects; RED-for-right-reason first; one RED->GREEN->REFACTOR.

steps.name: Clear/mark trial requests as fulfilled in the admin panel
criteria: Migration 0003_leads_fulfilled_at.sql adds a nullable fulfilled_at timestamptz to leads + an authenticated UPDATE RLS policy (USER applies it manually in Supabase, like the 5123 migrations)

Each trial request in the dashboard has a 'Mark as sent' action; clicking it sets fulfilled_at=now via an authenticated UPDATE and the request leaves the active to-action list (shown as sent / de-emphasized)
The active trial-requests list shows only unfulfilled (fulfilled_at null) requests; a fulfilled one does not reappear after reload
If the UPDATE fails (e.g. migration not yet applied), the UI shows a non-blocking error and does NOT crash or falsely mark it sent
bun run test green; bun run build clean; eslint clean
steps.scenario_name: Maintainer marks a trial request as sent and it clears from the active list (dashboard-survey-view.feature)
steps.implementation_notes: USER REQUEST 2026-05-31: 'clear' trial requests in the admin panel so we know we shipped them a license. MARK as fulfilled (keep the record), do NOT delete. READ FIRST: supabase/migrations/0002_assessment_leads.sql (leads table + authenticated SELECT policy - mirror its style for the new UPDATE policy), src/features/assessment/adapters/supabaseDashboardRepository.ts (load('user-survey') maps leads -> DashboardSurveyTrialRequest; authenticated client), src/features/assessment/components/AdminDashboard.tsx (SurveyTrialRequestsView), src/pages/AdminAssessment.tsx (loads + passes survey props; wire the new callback here). DESIGN: (a) migration 0003_leads_fulfilled_at.sql: `alter table public.leads add column if not exists fulfilled_at timestamptz;` + `create policy leads_authenticated_update on public.leads for update to authenticated using (true) with check (true);` (authenticated = the trusted benjamin@/peter@ dashboard accounts; mirrors the authenticated SELECT trust model - acceptable for an internal admin action). USER MANUAL STEP: apply this in the Supabase SQL editor (flag it like AI-2). (b) ports: DashboardSurveyTrialRequest gains `id` + `fulfilledAt: string | null`; DashboardRepository gains `markTrialFulfilled(id: string): Promise<void>`. (c) repository: load also selects id + fulfilled_at for user-survey-trial leads; markTrialFulfilled = authenticated `update({ fulfilled_at: new ISO }).eq('id', id)` (throw on error). (d) AdminDashboard SurveyTrialRequestsView: show only fulfilled_at===null requests in the active list with a 'Mark as sent' button per row; an onMarkTrialFulfilled(id) callback prop drives it; on success the row leaves the active list (optimistic update or reload). (e) AdminAssessment: pass onMarkTrialFulfilled={(id)=>repository.markTrialFulfilled(id).then(reload)} - reload the user-survey data after success; keep `now` stable (React #185). Degrade: if markTrialFulfilled rejects, surface a non-blocking error (role=alert), do NOT optimistically remove the row permanently. Strict TS no any; immutable; <=2 nesting; no comments. CI-LEARNINGS PRE-APPLY. RED handoff: SurveyDashboard.fulfill.test.tsx through the public AdminDashboard render with an injected onMarkTrialFulfilled double + a fulfilled vs unfulfilled fixture: assert only unfulfilled show, clicking calls the callback with the id, the row clears on success, and a rejecting callback shows an error without removing the row; RED-for-right-reason first; one RED->GREEN->REFACTOR.

steps.name: Keep sent trial requests in the list with a sent indicator
criteria: Fulfilled (sent) trial requests REMAIN visible in the trial-requests list (no longer filtered out), shown with a green checkmark / 'Sent' indicator instead of the 'Mark as sent' button
Unfulfilled requests still show the 'Mark as sent' button; clicking it flips that row to the sent indicator (optimistic, confirmed by reload)
On UPDATE failure the row stays unfulfilled with its button and a non-blocking error (unchanged degrade behaviour)
bun run test green; bun run build clean; eslint clean
steps.scenario_name: Sent trial requests stay listed with a sent checkmark (dashboard-survey-view.feature)
steps.implementation_notes: USER FEEDBACK 2026-05-31: instead of REMOVING a request from the list when marked sent (03-06 behaviour), KEEP it in the list with a green checkmark / 'Sent' indicator. READ FIRST: src/features/assessment/components/AdminDashboard.tsx (SurveyTrialRequestsView from 03-06 - it currently filters to fulfilledAt===null + tracks optimistically-removed ids). CHANGE: stop filtering out fulfilled rows - render ALL trial requests; for fulfilledAt!==null show a green check + 'Sent' (optionally the sent date via fulfilledAt) and NO button; for fulfilledAt===null show the 'Mark as sent' button. Replace the optimistic-REMOVAL set with an optimistic-FULFILL (locally set the row's fulfilledAt on click so it flips to the sent indicator immediately; the page reload then confirms it). On rejection revert to unfulfilled + the role=alert error (keep 03-06 degrade behaviour). Optional nicety: sort unfulfilled (actionable) before sent. Keep markTrialFulfilled / migration / ports unchanged. Strict TS no any; immutable; <=2 nesting; no comments. Use an accessible indicator (e.g. an aria-label/text 'Sent', not color alone). CI-LEARNINGS PRE-APPLY. RED handoff: update SurveyDashboard.fulfill.test.tsx - a fulfilled fixture row renders WITH a 'Sent' indicator and NO button; an unfulfilled row shows the button; clicking flips it to Sent (still listed); rejection keeps it unfulfilled with an error. RED-for-right-reason first; one RED->GREEN->REFACTOR.

phases.name: Slice 04 — in-app nudge visible for eligible non-premium users (US-06)
phases.description: LIGHTHOUSE repo (.NET 8 + React 18). Per-instance installTimestamp on AppSettings; FE-derived eligibility (premium-first/fail-closed/UTC-stable); nudge shown for non-premium, install age >= ~14d.

steps.name: Per-instance install timestamp + non-admin settings read
criteria: AppSettingKeys gains a write-once installTimestamp; AppSettingService.EnsureInstallTimestamp sets it once on first run; a non-admin read surface exposes it (existing AppSettingsController is [RbacGuard]-admin)
EF migration generated via the CreateMigration PowerShell script (Sqlite + Postgres); dotnet build zero-warning; dotnet test green
Startup probe verifies write-once durability + UTC read-after-write; fault degrades to not-eligible (fail closed)
steps.scenario_name: install timestamp is write-once and readable (nudge-eligibility.feature)
steps.implementation_notes: REFINE WHEN REACHED. LIGHTHOUSE repo (OOP, NUnit+EF). ADR-045. Use CreateMigration script (NOT dotnet ef migrations add). AI-4/AI-6.

steps.name: FE-derived eligibility + nudge component (premium-first/fail-closed)
criteria: A pure eligibility fn returns show only if not premium AND install age >= ~14d (UTC-stable) AND not recently shown; premium evaluated FIRST and absolute; any uncertainty -> fail closed (no nudge)
A deterministic test asserts a premium instance NEVER renders the nudge at any install age (guardrail KPI 5 = 0)
The nudge component (Lighthouse design system) is non-blocking, dismissible, links out to /survey; pnpm test green; pnpm build clean (Biome)
steps.scenario_name: premium never nudged; <14d no nudge; >=14d non-premium shown (nudge-eligibility.feature)
steps.implementation_notes: REFINE WHEN REACHED. LIGHTHOUSE FE (React 18 + Vitest + Biome). ADR-044 FE-derived (user-confirmed). @property via fast-check candidate for the boundary/premium guard.

phases.name: Slice 05 — nudge cadence + persisted dismissal (US-07)
phases.description: lastShownAt persisted server-side; ~6-month cadence; dismiss + click-through both set lastShownAt; no reappear until +~6mo; links to /survey.

steps.name: lastShownAt cadence + persisted dismissal
criteria: lastShownAt persisted server-side on show AND dismiss; nudge does not reappear until lastShownAt + ~6 months; click-through opens /survey and also sets lastShownAt
nudge-cadence-dismissal.feature scenarios pass; pnpm test green; pnpm build clean; dotnet build/test green
steps.scenario_name: dismiss/click-through persist lastShownAt; no reappear until +6mo (nudge-cadence-dismissal.feature)
steps.implementation_notes: REFINE WHEN REACHED. ADR-045 lastShownAt; both paths set/confirm lastShownAt.

validation.approved_by: orchestrator (slice-01 detailed; slices 02-05 coarse, refined when reached)
validation.notes: Slice 01 (phase 01) is fully decomposed and is the only slice executed now; user reviews before push. Phases 02-05 carry headline steps to be refined at slice start. Per ADR-046, slice-01 brief's anon-INSERT is superseded by the service_role submit-survey edge fn.
